name: Aunt Helen's "Killer" Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 3 cups fresh peaches, peeled, sliced into quarters
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the topping first: Mix flour, 1/2 cup white sugar, and baking powder. Add milk and 1/4 cup softened butter. Stir until all lumps are gone. Set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan, stir together brown sugar and cornstarch; add cold water. Gradually add peaches and blueberries. Cook and stir gently over medium heat until bubbly. Add lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of butter, stirring until the butter melts.
3. Pour the mixture into an ungreased baking dish. Spoon mounds of topping over the hot filling.
4. Sprinkle with a mixture of remaining white sugar, ground nutmeg, and ground cinnamon.
5. Place the baking dish on top of a cookie sheet to avoid spillover in the oven.
6. Bake cobbler in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the crust comes out clean.
7. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream, or if you are feeling frisky, whipped cream. If blueberries are not available, add one additional cup of peaches to the recipe.
